fever: Rest and drink plenty of fluids.
Monitor temperature and seek care if it stays high for more than 48 hours.
high_fever: Rest, hydrate, and use cool compresses.
Seek medical advice if fever is very high or persistent.
persistent_cough: If cough lasts more than 2-3 weeks, see a doctor.
shortness_of_breath: Seek urgent care if breathing is difficult or worsening.
breathing_difficulty: Seek urgent care if breathing is difficult or worsening.
wheezing: Avoid triggers like smoke or dust.
Seek medical care if wheezing is severe or persistent.
headache: Rest in a quiet, dark room and hydrate.
severe_headache: Seek urgent medical evaluation, especially if sudden or unusual.
dizziness: Sit or lie down and hydrate.
fainting: Lie down and elevate legs if safe.
Seek medical care if it happens again.
nausea: Take small sips of clear fluids.
Eat light meals and avoid oily foods.
vomiting: Take small sips of oral rehydration solution.
diarrhea: Use oral rehydration solution and stay hydrated.
Seek care if dehydration or blood in stool occurs.
dehydration: Seek care if severe or unable to keep fluids down.
rash: Keep skin clean and avoid scratching.
Seek care if rash spreads quickly or with fever.
skin_rash: Keep skin clean and avoid scratching.
Seek care if rash spreads quickly or with fever.
hives: Seek urgent care if breathing difficulty or swelling occurs.
swelling_face: Seek urgent care if swelling is sudden or severe.
seizures: Keep the person safe and turn them on their side.
Call emergency services if seizure lasts more than 5 minutes.
severe_bleeding: Apply firm pressure to the wound and seek emergency care.
uncontrolled_bleeding: Apply firm pressure and seek emergency care immediately.
burns: Cool the burn under running water for several minutes.
Seek care if the burn is severe or extensive.
